key_predictive_features: Gold/silver ratio velocity (ROC of ratio) — mean reversion predictor
COT positioning: speculative net longs vs commercial shorts
Real yield (TIPS breakeven) — correlation broke post-2022 but still useful at extremes
Industrial demand proxies: solar panel installations, copper prices
Central bank gold buying (proxy for precious metals demand)
DXY inverse correlation — stronger predictor than individual technicals
Copper/gold ratio as economic health proxy
fundamental_drivers: Silver supply deficit: mine output declining, industrial demand rising
Solar panel demand: each GW of solar capacity uses ~20 tonnes of silver
Electric vehicle battery demand (silver content in connectors)
Central bank diversification away from USD reserves

cross_asset_correlations.gold: Strongest — G/S ratio compression thesis active
cross_asset_correlations.copper: Leading indicator for industrial silver demand
cross_asset_correlations.VIX: Mixed — safe haven vs risk-off industrial demand destruction
cross_asset_correlations.oil: Moderate — energy costs affect mining supply

seasonal_patterns: January effect: silver tends to rally in Jan-Feb (jewelry/industrial restocking)
September-October weakness: seasonal mine supply increase
Indian wedding season (Oct-Dec) drives gold demand, pulls silver
papers_and_models: IJISRT 2024-2025: SVR-RBF best for gold, ELM competitive for silver
JPMorgan: 100 tonnes CB gold buying = 1.7% gold price increase (silver follows with leverage)
Tree-based classifiers (RF, XGBoost) best directional accuracy for precious metals ETFs
recommended_new_signals: Gold/silver ratio ROC (rate of change) — mean reversion signal
Central bank flow quarterly update — medium-term directional bias
DXY momentum as cross-asset predictor (already have dxy_cross_asset, disabled)
Copper price momentum as leading indicator
LightGBM meta-learner on existing 12 metals signal outputs
deep_dives.implementation_notes: XAG accuracy at coin-flip level suggests individual signals cancel out. A meta-learner could capture non-linear interactions between signals that simple voting misses.

key_predictive_features: BTC lead-lag: BTC moves lead ETH by 1-4 hours (exploitable with hourly data)
ETH/BTC ratio momentum — ratio expansion/compression predicts relative performance
DeFi TVL changes — proxy for ETH demand
Gas fee trends — high fees = high network activity = demand
Staking yield changes — yield compression may reduce buy pressure
Layer 2 activity (Arbitrum, Optimism, Base) — ETH demand proxy
fundamental_drivers: Ethereum staking: 26%+ of ETH locked, supply squeeze
DeFi protocol revenue as ETH demand driver
Layer 2 adoption reducing gas fees but increasing ETH settlement demand
Pectra upgrade and future scaling roadmap

cross_asset_correlations.BTC: Strongest — 0.85+ correlation, BTC leads by hours
cross_asset_correlations.risk_assets: Trades like high-beta tech stock in macro regimes
cross_asset_correlations.gas_fees: Internal correlation — high gas = more ETH burned

seasonal_patterns: Known noise: ETH Now-TF oscillates rapidly (documented as noise pattern)
Post-upgrade rallies tend to fade within 2-4 weeks
Funding rate sustained negative periods predict short squeezes
papers_and_models: CNN-LSTM on composite on-chain features: 82-84% for cycle positioning
MDPI Finance 2025: funding rate regime shifts Granger-cause price shocks
ETH/BTC ratio as cointegrated pair for statistical arbitrage
recommended_new_signals: BTC lead signal: use BTC 1h-4h price changes to predict ETH direction
DeFi TVL momentum (already in crypto_macro, but collapsed to 34% recently)
ETH/BTC ratio z-score for mean reversion
Funding rate regime transition detector (CUSUM-based, not level-based)
Gas fee velocity as network demand proxy
deep_dives.implementation_notes: ETH accuracy is near coin-flip because ETH largely follows BTC with noise. A BTC-lead signal exploiting the 1-4h lag could be the single most impactful improvement.
